items.title: Personally I don't think the Pelicans should deal with Detroit unless Ausar is on the table. And if he's not, there needs to be serious draft capital. Talking 3+ with swaps on top.
items.author: Shamit Dua
items.body_excerpt:
Personally I don't think the Pelicans should deal with Detroit unless Ausar is on the table. And if he's not, there needs to be serious draft capital. Talking 3+ with swaps on top.

Indiana doesn't have draft capital but what can their players net from a third team?

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Chuck Nevitt. NC St big had 5.5 ppg, was drafted 63rd by HOU in 82. Could block shots but was unskilled on O & not strong for his size. Still, lasted 11 yrs as deep bench C. Teams constantly tried to fatten him up. Now works in IT.

items.body_excerpt:

Allen Iverson scored 35 PTS to lead the 76ers, who trail Los Angeles in the series, 3-1.

items.title: Incredible read on Jose Alvarado from @jamesherbert.bsky.social: www.cbssports.com/nba/news/kni...
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: Incredible read on Jose Alvarado from @jamesherbert.bsky.social: www.cbssports.com/nba/news/kni...

images.alt:
One of Pannone's mentors, the coach/skills trainer/analyst David Thorpe, had taught him that when smaller players are successful, it means they've figured something out that others haven't. That Alvarado had earned such an award at his height, then, suggested he was special. 

Later, Alvarado worked out for the Pelicans, which gave the team a chance to sit down with him. Often, since prospects are coached for these sessions, they are not particularly revealing. Alvarado's, however, made New Orleans' management fall in love with him. The highlight was when, during an exercise with the team's mental performance coach, he was asked, "What's your spirit animal?"

Alvarado stood up from his chair. He beat his chest, let out a scream and said, "Gorilla."
